intitule: Exercise 1: Identify the Present Perfect Tense (Resource)

enonce.texte: Instructions: Read the sentences below. Identify whether the verb is in the Present Perfect tense or Simple Past tense. Then, explain why the Present Perfect is used (experience, unfinished past, or recent event with no time given).

questions.texte: She has visited the dentist twice this year.
questions.reponse: Present Perfect. Reason: Unfinished past (this year is not finished).

questions.texte: They went to Limbe last weekend.
questions.reponse: Simple Past. Reason: Specific past time (last weekend).

questions.texte: I have never eaten eru with plantain.
questions.reponse: Present Perfect. Reason: Experience (from birth until now).

questions.texte: He has just finished his homework.
questions.reponse: Present Perfect. Reason: Recent event with 'just' (no specific time).

enonce.texte: Instructions: Complete the following sentences using the Present Perfect form of the verbs in parentheses. Pay attention to the subject (have/has) and irregular verbs.

questions.texte: The government (build) __________ three new hospitals in the North Region since 2020.
We (not/see) __________ any cases of cholera in our village this month.
My mother (lose) __________ her keys. She cannot open the door.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Rewrite the following sentences from the Simple Past to the Present Perfect. Change the time expression if necessary. Use the words in brackets.

questions.texte: I bought fresh fish at the Mfoundi market yesterday. (just)
questions.reponse: I have just bought fresh fish at the Mfoundi market.

questions.texte: They learned about HIV/AIDS in 2019. (never / before)
questions.reponse: They have never learned about HIV/AIDS before.

questions.texte: Drought destroyed many crops in the Far North last year. (several times / since 2015)
questions.reponse: Drought has destroyed many crops in the Far North several times since 2015.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Read the dialogue between two students in Yaoundé. Then, answer the questions below using the Present Perfect tense. Justify your answers.

questions.texte:
A: 'I feel very tired and my throat hurts.'
B: 'Have you seen the school nurse?'
A: 'No, not yet.'

According to the dialogue, what action has NOT happened?
questions.reponse: The student has not seen the school nurse.

questions.texte:
Your friend tells you: 'I have never eaten a balanced diet.'
As a health prefect, what advice can you give him using the Present Perfect? (Start with 'You should... because...')
questions.reponse: You should start eating vegetables and fruits because many studies have shown that a balanced diet prevents diseases like high blood pressure.

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Your school has just organized a 'Clean Environment Campaign'. Write a short report (4-5 sentences) for the school newspaper. You must use the Present Perfect tense at least 3 times to describe:
1. What students have done (e.g., collect waste).
2. What the school has achieved.
3. A problem that has not been solved yet.

questions.reponse: Example answer: Our school has organized a successful Clean Environment Campaign. Students have collected over 50 kg of plastic bottles and waste around the classrooms. The administration has planted 20 new trees to fight deforestation. However, we have not yet solved the problem of waste burning near the canteen.
